transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.text: Let's use the board. Explain how photosynthesis works. Start with one claim, then evidence.

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.text: I can explain part of it, but I might mix up the mechanism.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.text: Good start. What on the board supports that claim, and what could challenge it?

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.text: I think sunlight turns directly into oxygen, so maybe that is the main mechanism.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.text: Notice the step near your diagram: chlorophyll captures light energy. Why does that make sunlight turns directly into oxygen risky?

transcript.speaker: Student
transcript.text: I am still stuck. I need a smaller hint before I can repair that.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.text: Nice revision. Try one final sentence that connects the evidence to the mechanism.

element.text: Maybe: sunlight turns directly into oxygen

drawings:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="960" height="640" viewBox="0 0 960 640">
  <defs>
    <marker id="arrow" markerWidth="10" markerHeight="10" refX="8" refY="3" orient="auto">
      <path d="M0,0 L0,6 L9,3 z" fill="currentColor"/>
    </marker>
  </defs>
  <text x="72" y="84" font-size="30" fill="oklch(27% 0.008 70)">CO2 + H2O + light -> glucose + O2</text>
  <text x="72" y="150" font-size="24" fill="oklch(27% 0.008 70)">Claim: chlorophyll captures light energy</text>
  <line x1="100" y1="170" x2="100" y2="220" stroke="oklch(61% 0.115 42)" stroke-width="3" stroke-linecap="round" marker-end="url(#arrow)"/>
  <text x="72" y="252" font-size="22" fill="oklch(27% 0.008 70)">Maybe: sunlight turns directly into oxygen</text>
  <text x="72" y="330" font-size="24" fill="oklch(50% 0.12 240)">Check the input/output relationship</text>
  <ellipse cx="318" cy="251" rx="260" ry="31" fill="none" stroke="oklch(61% 0.115 42)" stroke-width="3"/>
</svg>
